tasks.title: Prevent manifest truncation during limited or partial discovery
tasks.goal: Preserve the canonical manifest inventory when discovery is intentionally limited or temporarily incomplete.
scope: Define canonical-manifest behavior for discover-pleno and sync-pleno when --limit is used.
Preserve previously known records and their download state during partial rediscovery.
Add regression coverage for full-to-limited discovery sequences.
acceptance_criteria: discover-pleno and sync-pleno preserve previously known manifest entries when --limit is used or discovery returns a temporary subset.
Regression tests cover a full discovery followed by a limited discovery without truncating canonical inventory.
Existing download state for unaffected records remains intact after reconciliation.
tests: uv run pytest tests/test_manifest.py tests/test_services.py tests/test_cli.py
tasks.notes: Assumption: --limit is an operator sampling/control surface and must not destructively rewrite the canonical manifest.

tasks.title: Make manifest persistence atomic and resilient to partial writes
tasks.goal: Ensure manifest write and load paths are safe against interruption and malformed persisted state.
scope: Implement temp-file-plus-replace persistence for JSONL and CSV outputs.
Define the corruption-handling contract for malformed manifest input.
Add tests that cover interrupted or malformed persistence scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest JSONL and CSV writes use a temp-file-plus-replace strategy so interrupted writes do not leave partial canonical files.
Malformed manifest input follows a documented error contract with actionable operator guidance and automated test coverage.
Tests prove manifest persistence remains consistent across write and load failure scenarios.
tasks.rollout: Land after canonical inventory semantics are fixed so persistence safeguards align with the intended merge behavior.

tasks.title: Capture HTTP failure metadata for failed PDF downloads
tasks.goal: Persist actionable failure metadata so operators can distinguish HTTP, transport, and validation failures for PDF downloads.
scope: Preserve HTTP status or transport classification on failure paths when available.
Extend persisted download metadata and logging as needed without regressing successful downloads.
Add failure-path tests for HTTP and non-HTTP errors.
acceptance_criteria: Failed downloads persist structured HTTP or transport metadata when available, not only a free-form error message.
Automated tests cover at least one HTTP failure path and one non-HTTP transport failure path.
Operator-facing logs or manifest fields allow distinguishing status-code failures from network or validation failures.
tasks.observability: Download failures should remain queryable from manifest outputs and JSONL logs without inspecting stack traces.

tasks.title: Clarify or normalize missing parliamentary metadata in parsed records
tasks.goal: Make the parser contract explicit for missing periodo_anual and legislatura values so downstream consumers can rely on it.
scope: Choose between nullable output, deterministic backfill, or another documented policy.
Enforce the chosen behavior in parser logic and fixture-backed tests.
Document the contract for downstream consumers and operators.
acceptance_criteria: The data contract explicitly defines whether periodo_anual and legislatura are nullable or backfilled.
Parser behavior and fixtures are covered by tests for rows with missing hierarchy metadata.
User-facing documentation records the chosen policy so downstream consumers can rely on it.
tasks.notes: Assumption: current fixture omissions may be source-authentic, so the contract must tolerate them unless the team decides on verified backfill rules.
